description:
GOAL: Dead-fn elimination pass that removes FnIrs with zero remaining
callsites after inlining (or that are otherwise unreachable from `main`).

Status when authored: scoped out of fz-ul4.11.15. The inliner intentionally
leaves callee FnIrs in `module.fns` even after all their callsites are
inlined away; codegen still compiles them, wasting cycles and code space.

## What "reachable" means in fz-IR

  Starting from `main` FnIr:
    - Follow Term::Call.callee → FnId.
    - Follow Term::TailCall.callee → FnId.
    - Follow Term::Call.continuation.fn_id → FnId.
    - Follow Term::CallClosure.continuation.fn_id → FnId.
    - Follow Prim::MakeClosure(FnId, _) → FnId.
  Mark all reached FnIds; sweep the rest.

## Schema-vec interaction

  ir_codegen builds `schemas` as `vec![placeholder; max_id + 1]` indexed by
  FnId.0 (codegen.rs:1473-1480). If DCE drops fns, the schemas vec STAYS the
  same size (we don't renumber FnIds) — placeholder entries are harmless.
  Equivalently, we can compact by remapping FnIds, but renumbering all
  call/cont/closure refs is more invasive. v1 DCE: just drop from
  module.fns; keep FnIds dense at max_id.

## Tests

  - After inline_module, a fully-inlined leaf callee with 0 remaining
    callsites is dropped from module.fns.
  - A fn still referenced via Prim::MakeClosure (even if MakeClosure result
    is unused) is kept (conservative).
  - main's FnIr is always kept.
  - End-to-end: SAMPLE output unchanged.

## Dependencies

  - Blocked by fz-ul4.11.15 (DCE only matters after inliner exists).

## Acceptance

  - One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.28.
title: Dead-callee elimination pass
